{{Infobox character
|nom=Five
|apparition=Appears in book 1
|status=Unknown
}}

== Biography ==

Five is one of three gardeners in the Queen's garden tasked with painting white roses red to conceal a planting error before the Queen's discovery. He exhibits a sulky disposition and characteristically deflects blame for his own mistakes; when his painting splashes, he attributes the incident to Seven jogging his elbow rather than acknowledging his own carelessness.

== Relationships ==

'''[[Alice]]''' — ally. Five's connection with Alice appears to form through her presence at the gardeners' work.

'''[[Queen]]''' — enemy. The Queen represents the threat of execution should she discover their planting error.

'''[[Seven]]''' — acquaintance. Seven and Five exchange accusations over the spilled paint, each deflecting blame.

'''[[Two]]''' — ally. Two works alongside Five on the task of repainting the roses.

''Evolution:''
* [[Alice]] — Ally (ch.8)
* [[Queen]] — Enemy (ch.8)
* [[Seven]] — Acquaintance (ch.8)
* [[Two]] — Ally (ch.8)


[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Personnages du Tome 1]]
